title: New Mexico bets big on emerging quantum computing industry
why:
- New Mexico is leading the nation in quantum computing, supported by Los Alamos and Sandia National Labs and the highly regarded Quantum Institute at the University of New Mexico.
- State lawmakers approved over $100 million in incentives in 2024 to attract more quantum companies, such as Qunnect, which opened the U.S.’s first open-ended quantum network in Albuquerque.
- Experts project quantum computing to become a $1 trillion industry by 2035.
- New Mexico and Colorado were designated in 2024 as the first federally recognized quantum hub in the United States.
- The quantum industry in New Mexico is expected to create hundreds, potentially thousands, of jobs in the near future.
- There are discussions about creating an undergraduate quantum degree at the University of New Mexico to build a qualified workforce.
